purpose: Define the required fields, source reference rules, and generation semantics for plugin package catalog files.
file_format_notes: Catalog files are authored in YAML with the .yaml extension. The contract document itself remains JSON; it describes catalog file shape, it is not a catalog file.

plugins.plugin_root_rule: Path must stay inside the declared bucket, normally <bucket>/plugins/<id>.
plugins.component_value_rule: Component values are shared/ source paths, not IDs that require separate component catalog files.

true_means: Generated output must contain only catalog-declared components.
Every generated file must map back to a declared source_ref, template, or output mapping.
false_means: Generated output may include extra platform scaffolding or compatibility files.
Extra files must be listed in generation.allowed_extra_outputs or be clearly marked generated.
Missing source_refs are still validation errors.

rules: Inline content must be deterministic and validation-covered.

validation_rules: Every catalog file must have all common_required_fields.
The filename stem must match id.
The containing catalog directory must be catalog/plugins.
source_refs must exist and must not point under generated/.
catalog/plugins entries must use a known marketplace bucket.
catalog/plugins plugin_root must stay inside the declared bucket.
Component values referenced from catalog/plugins must be shared/ source paths.
Catalog plugin files must use the .yaml extension and parse as valid YAML.
Component catalog roots such as catalog/agents or catalog/mcp are invalid unless a future contract explicitly adds them.
Target platform mappings must be valid according to the Claude/Codex platform_component_support matrix.
Strict generation must reject undeclared generated components and files.
